info.brief: Monarch is a screen manager for the Defold game engine.
info.description: Monarch is a screen manager for the Defold game engine.

elements.name: TRANSITION
types: { DONE: Hash; SHOW_IN: Hash; SHOW_OUT: Hash; BACK_IN: Hash; BACK_OUT: Hash; }

elements.name: debug
elements.brief: Enable verbose logging of the internals of Monarch.
elements.description: Enable verbose logging of the internals of Monarch.

elements.name: in_stack
elements.brief: Check if a screen exists in the current screen stack
elements.description: Check if a screen exists in the current screen stack

returnvalues.doc: true of the screen is in the stack

elements.name: is_top
elements.brief: Check if a Monarch screen is at the top of the view stack.
elements.description: Check if a Monarch screen is at the top of the view stack.

returnvalues.doc: true if the screen is at the top of the stack

elements.name: is_visible
elements.brief: Check if a Monarch screen is visible.
elements.description: Check if a Monarch screen is visible.

returnvalues.doc: true if the screen is visible

elements.name: is_loaded
elements.brief: Check if a screen is loaded
elements.description: Check if a screen is loaded

returnvalues.doc: true if the screen is loaded

elements.name: is_popup
elements.brief: Check if a screen is a popup
elements.description: Check if a screen is a popup

returnvalues.doc: true if the screen is a popup

elements.name: register_proxy
elements.brief: Register a new screen contained in a collection proxy.
elements.description:
Register a new screen contained in a collection proxy.
This is done automatically by the screen_proxy.script. It is expected that
the caller of this function is a script component attached to the same game
object as the proxy. This is required since monarch will acquire and
release input focus of the game object where the proxy is attached.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Unique id of the screen

parameters.name: proxy
parameters.doc: URL to the collection proxy containing the screen

parameters.name: settings
parameters.doc:
Settings table for screen. Accepted values:
* popup - true if the screen is a popup
* popup_on_popup - true if this popup can be shown on top of
another popup or false if an underlying popup should be closed
* timestep_below_popup - Timestep to set on proxy when below a popup
* screen_keeps_input_focus_when_below_popup - If this screen should
keep input focus when below a popup
* others_keep_input_focus_when_below_screen - If screens below this
screen should keep input focus
* auto_preload - true if the screen should be automatically preloaded

elements.name: register_factory
elements.brief: Register a new screen contained in a collection factory.
elements.description:
Register a new screen contained in a collection factory.
This is done automatically by the screen_factory.script. It is expected that
the caller of this function is a script component attached to the same game
object as the factory. This is required since monarch will acquire and
release input focus of the game object where the factory is attached.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Unique id of the screen

parameters.name: factory
parameters.doc: URL to the collection factory containing the screen

parameters.name: settings
parameters.doc:
Settings table for screen. Accepted values:
* popup - true if the screen is a popup
* popup_on_popup - true if this popup can be shown on top of
another popup or false if an underlying popup should be closed
* screen_keeps_input_focus_when_below_popup - If this screen should
keep input focus when below a popup
* others_keep_input_focus_when_below_screen - If screens below this
screen should keep input focus
* auto_preload - true if the screen should be automatically preloaded

elements.name: unregister
elements.brief: Unregister a screen
elements.description:
Unregister a screen
This is done automatically by the screen.script

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to unregister

elements.name: data
elements.brief: Get data associated with a screen
elements.description: Get data associated with a screen

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to get data for

elements.name: screen_exists
elements.brief: Check if a Monarch screen exists.
elements.description: Check if a Monarch screen exists.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to check if is registered

returnvalues.doc: True or False if the screen id is registered or not

elements.name: is_busy
elements.brief: Check if Monarch is busy showing and/or hiding a screen.
elements.description: Check if Monarch is busy showing and/or hiding a screen.

elements.name: show
elements.brief: Show a Monarch screen.
elements.description: Show a Monarch screen.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to check if is registered

parameters.name: options
parameters.doc:
Table with options when showing the screen (can be nil). Valid values:
- clear - Set to true if the stack should be cleared down to an existing instance of the screen
- reload - Set to true if screen should be reloaded if it already exists in the stack and is loaded.
This would be the case if doing a show() from a popup on the screen just below the popup.
- sequential - Set to true to wait for the previous screen to show itself out before starting the
show in transition even when transitioning to a different scene ID.
- no_stack - Set to true to load the screen without adding it to the screen stack.
- pop - The number of screens to pop from the stack before adding the new one.

parameters.name: data
parameters.doc: Optional data to set on the screen. Can be retrieved by the `data()` function

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when screen is shown

elements.name: replace
elements.brief: Replace the top of the stack with a new screen.
elements.description: Replace the top of the stack with a new screen.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to show.

parameters.name: options
parameters.doc:
Table with options when showing the screen (can be nil). Valid values:
- clear {boolean} - Set to true if the stack should be cleared down to an existing instance of the screen.
- reload {boolean} - Set to true if the screen should be reloaded if it already exists in the stack and is loaded.
This would be the case if doing a show() from a popup on the screen just below the popup.
- no_stack {boolean} - Set to true to load the screen without adding it to the screen stack.

parameters.name: data
parameters.doc: Optional data to set on the screen. Can be retrieved by the data() function.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the screen is shown.

elements.name: hide
elements.brief: Hide a screen. The screen must either be at the top of the stack or
elements.description:
Hide a screen. The screen must either be at the top of the stack or
visible but not added to the stack (through the no_stack option).

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to hide.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the screen is hidden.

returnvalues.doc: True if successfully hiding, false if busy or for some other reason unable to hide the screen.

elements.name: clear
elements.brief: Clear the stack completely. Any visible screens will be hidden by navigating back out
elements.description:
Clear the stack completely. Any visible screens will be hidden by navigating back out
from them.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the stack has been cleared.

elements.name: back
elements.brief: Go back to the previous screen in the stack.
elements.description: Go back to the previous screen in the stack.

parameters.name: options
parameters.doc:
Table with options when backing out from the screen (can be nil).
Valid values:
- sequential {boolean} - Set to true to wait for the current screen to hide itself out before starting the
back in transition even when transitioning to a different scene ID.

parameters.name: data
parameters.doc: Optional data to set for the previous screen.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the previous screen is visible again.

elements.name: is_preloading
elements.brief: Check if a screen is preloading via monarch.preload() or automatically
elements.description:
Check if a screen is preloading via monarch.preload() or automatically
via the Preload screen option.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Screen id.

elements.name: when_preloaded
elements.brief: Invoke a callback when a specific screen has been preloaded.
elements.description:
Invoke a callback when a specific screen has been preloaded.
This is mainly useful on app start when wanting to show a screen that
has the `Preload` flag set (since it will immediately start to load which
would prevent a call to `monarch.show` from having any effect).

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to monitor for preload completion.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Callback to invoke when the screen has been preloaded.

elements.name: preload
elements.brief: Preload a screen. This will load but not enable and show a screen.
elements.description:
Preload a screen. This will load but not enable and show a screen.
Useful for "heavier" screens that you wish to show without any delay.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to preload.

parameters.name: options
parameters.doc: Options for preloading the screen.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the screen is loaded.

elements.name: has_missing_resources
elements.brief: Check if a screen has missing resources, always returns false for factory
elements.description: Check if a screen has missing resources, always returns false for factory

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to preload

elements.name: unload
elements.brief: Unload a preloaded monarch screen.
elements.description: Unload a preloaded monarch screen.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to unload.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.doc: Optional callback to invoke when the screen is unloaded.

elements.name: post
elements.brief: Post a message to a screen using msg.post.
elements.description: Post a message to a screen using msg.post.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to send the message to.

parameters.name: message_id
parameters.doc: Id of the message to send.

parameters.name: message
parameters.doc: Optional message data to send.

returnvalues.doc: result - True if the message is sent successfully.

elements.name: get_stack
elements.brief: Get a list of IDs for the current screen stack.
elements.description:
Get a list of IDs for the current screen stack.
(Primarily used for unit testing, but could have uses outside testing.)

returnvalues.doc:
Table with screen IDs. The first entry is at the bottom of the
stack, and the last value is at the top (and currently visible).

elements.name: top
elements.brief: Get the screen on top of the stack.
elements.description: Get the screen on top of the stack.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.doc: Optional offset from the top of the stack (e.g., -1 for the previous screen).

elements.name: bottom
elements.brief: Get the screen at the bottom of the stack.
elements.description: Get the screen at the bottom of the stack.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.doc: Optional offset from the bottom of the stack.

elements.name: set_timestep_below_popup
elements.brief: Set the timestep to apply for a screen when below a popup.
elements.description: Set the timestep to apply for a screen when below a popup.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Id of the screen to change the timestep setting for.

parameters.name: timestep
parameters.doc: Timestep to apply.

elements.name: on_transition
elements.brief: Set a function to call when a transition should be started.
elements.description:
Set a function to call when a transition should be started.
The function will receive (`message_id`, `message`, `sender`).
IMPORTANT! You must call `monarch.on_message()` from the same script as
this function was called.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Screen id to associate the transition with.

parameters.name: fn
parameters.doc: Transition handler function.
types: (message_id: string, message: Message, sender: Url) => void

elements.name: on_focus_changed
elements.brief: Set a function to call when a screen gains or loses focus.
elements.description:
Set a function to call when a screen gains or loses focus.
The function will receive (`message_id`, `message`, `sender`).
IMPORTANT! You must call `monarch.on_message()` from the same script as
this function was called.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Screen id to associate the focus listener function with.

parameters.name: fn
parameters.doc: Focus listener function.
types: (message_id: string, message: Message, sender: Url) => void

elements.name: on_post
elements.brief: Set either a function to be called when `msg.post()` is called on a specific screen
elements.description:
Set either a function to be called when `msg.post()` is called on a specific screen
or a URL where the message is sent.
IMPORTANT! If you provide a function, you must also make sure to call
`monarch.on_message(message_id, message, sender)` from the same script as
this function was called.

parameters.name: screen_id
parameters.doc: Screen id to associate the message listener function with.

parameters.name: fn_or_url
parameters.doc: The function to call or URL to send the message to.

elements.name: add_listener
elements.brief: Add a listener to be notified when screens are shown or hidden.
elements.description: Add a listener to be notified when screens are shown or hidden.

parameters.name: url
parameters.doc: The url to notify, undefined for the current url.

elements.name: remove_listener
elements.brief: Remove a previously added listener.
elements.description: Remove a previously added listener.

parameters.name: url
parameters.doc: The url to remove, nil for current url.
